artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2024-04-21
artifacts.excerpt:
@Drachs1978 It makes some sense, dude was doing it for fun and it exploded in just a couple of days, 50k a day is "instant-multi-millionaire status" in a place with super low-costs, & low security. He was receiving a ton of unwanted attention and freaked out.

I wonder what he has been up to

artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2024-04-21
artifacts.excerpt:
@Pt131200 @Drachs1978 The thing about security is that a considerable component of it is the *perception of* security, and the game got so viral that people were looking for him and telling others about his massive success.

From what I recall all this was forcing him into weird situations.

artifacts.title: Too receipt from 2024-03-29
artifacts.excerpt:
It annoys me tremendously when people say "I was depressed" as a code to "I was in a shitty context"

I recognize this in myself too, for a while in my early 20's I thought the depression was the problem... Actually it was a completely natural response

https://t.co/L6poJNEkBZ
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: too, yeah, many, bit.

artifacts.title: Too receipt from 2024-03-29
artifacts.excerpt:
to the context I was in. And healthy too!

but this raises a question: where does one draw the line? it must be drawn somewhere... because "depression doesn't exist" is just plain wrong
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: too, yeah, many, bit.

artifacts.title: Too receipt from 2024-03-29
artifacts.excerpt:
the one way out is accepting it, surrender to it, face your situation side-by-side with it, go on the journey it is forcing on you

but

you can't take it **too** seriously, because it is a unreliable narrator
